skill.name: Apple HIG: Status Components
skill.description: Apple Human Interface Guidelines for status and progress UI components including progress indicators, status bars, and activity rings.
skill.summary: Expert guidance on implementing progress indicators, status bars, and activity rings per Apple HIG standards.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 128 patterns but all are FALSE POSITIVES. Files are Markdown documentation referencing Apple Developer Documentation URLs. Backticks are Markdown inline code syntax (e.g., `HKActivityRingView`), not shell commands. URLs point to developer.apple.com. No executable code, network calls, or security risks exist.

content.user_title: Implement Apple HIG Status and Progress Components
content.value_statement: Uncertain about progress indicators, status bars, or activity rings? This skill provides authoritative Apple Human Interface Guidelines for status and progress UI components across iOS, iPadOS, watchOS, and macOS.
actual_capabilities: Recommends determinate vs indeterminate progress indicators based on operation duration
Provides status bar visibility and styling guidance for iOS and iPadOS
Explains activity ring implementation for watchOS fitness tracking
Delivers platform-specific best practices across Apple platforms
References official Apple Developer Documentation with accurate citations
Advises on accessibility considerations for progress announcements
limitations: Only covers Apple platforms (iOS, iPadOS, watchOS, macOS, tvOS, visionOS)
Does not provide implementation code, only design guidance
Requires manual verification for latest API changes
Cannot execute HealthKit queries or access device sensors

use_cases.title: Mobile App Loading States
use_cases.description: Determine appropriate progress indicator for network operations and background tasks in iOS apps
use_cases.target_user: iOS developers implementing download or upload features

use_cases.title: Fitness App Activity Tracking
use_cases.description: Implement activity rings that comply with Apple HIG for Move, Exercise, and Stand goals
use_cases.target_user: watchOS developers building health and fitness applications

use_cases.title: Immersive Media Experience
use_cases.description: Decide when to hide or show status bar for full-screen video, photos, or games
use_cases.target_user: UX designers creating immersive iOS experiences

prompt_templates.title: Basic Progress Indicator Selection
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to show progress for a file download. Should I use a progress bar or spinner? The download takes 10-30 seconds.

prompt_templates.title: Status Bar Visibility Decision
prompt_templates.prompt: I'm building a photo viewer. When should I hide the status bar and how do users bring it back?

prompt_templates.title: Activity Ring Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Can I use activity ring colors (red, green, blue) for my own custom progress visualization that is not related to fitness tracking?

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Multi-Platform Progress
prompt_templates.prompt: I need consistent progress feedback across iOS, iPadOS, and watchOS for a workout app. What are the platform-specific requirements and how do I handle activity ring display on each platform?

output_examples.input: Should I use a spinner or progress bar for a 5-second image upload?
output: Use a determinate progress bar for a 5-second upload.
Rationale: Determinate indicators show measurable progress and feel faster than spinners. Users can estimate remaining time.
HIG Reference: ProgressIndicators - 'Prefer determinate progress indicators when duration is known.'

output_examples.input: Is it okay to hide the status bar in my reading app?
output: Hide status bar only for immersive reading mode, not throughout the app.
Provide a tap gesture to restore status bar visibility.
Users need access to time and battery status without leaving your app.
HIG Reference: StatusBars - 'Avoid permanently hiding the status bar.'

best_practices: Use determinate progress bars when operation duration or percentage is known
Place progress indicators inline near content being loaded, not in distant modal dialogs
Maintain status bar visibility except for truly immersive experiences like full-screen video
anti_patterns: Using spinners for operations with known duration - users cannot estimate wait time
Stacking multiple progress indicators - aggregate into single representation
Repurposing activity ring colors or design for non-fitness data - violates HIG

faq.question: When should I use a spinner versus a progress bar?
faq.answer: Use a progress bar when you know the duration or percentage. Use a spinner (indeterminate) only when the duration is unknown, such as waiting for a network response with no size information.

faq.question: Can I customize activity ring colors for my fitness app?
faq.answer: No. Activity ring colors are fixed: red for Move, green for Exercise, blue for Stand. Never modify these colors or use the ring metaphor for unrelated data.

faq.question: Is it acceptable to hide the status bar in my game?
faq.answer: Yes, hiding the status bar is appropriate for immersive experiences like games and full-screen media. Provide a simple gesture to restore it if users need to check the time.

faq.question: How do I handle multiple simultaneous downloads?
faq.answer: Avoid stacking multiple indicators. Show aggregate progress (combined percentage) or display progress for the most relevant operation. Never overwhelm users with multiple spinners.

faq.question: What accessibility considerations apply to progress indicators?
faq.answer: Announce progress changes via VoiceOver using live regions. Provide percentage updates at meaningful intervals, not on every minor change. Ensure adequate color contrast.

faq.question: Can I use activity rings to display non-fitness data like task completion?
faq.answer: No. Activity rings are exclusively for Move, Exercise, and Stand goals. Using them for other data confuses users and violates Apple HIG. Use standard progress indicators instead.
